title: ProofMeta Signed Envelope
description: The single primitive of the ProofMeta protocol. Every manifest, license request, and status update is wrapped in a Signed Envelope. See PROOFMETA_ANWEISUNG.md §3.1.

payload.description: The actual content being signed. Shape depends on payload.type — see payload.manifest, payload.license-request, payload.status-update schemas.

type.description: Discriminator: 'manifest' | 'license.request' | 'status.update' (other values MAY appear for extension payload types).

payload_hash.description: sha256 of the JCS (RFC 8785) canonical serialization of payload. Lowercase hex.

author.description: DID identifying the signer. v1 MUST support did:key with ed25519 (see §3.1.2). Consumers MAY accept other DID methods by policy.

signature.description: ed25519 signature over the payload_hash string, produced by the author's key.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp asserted by the author.

in_reply_to.description: payload_hash of the logically previous envelope in the same lifecycle. Required for status updates and reviews. Omitted for roots (manifest, license.request).

anchors.description: Optional external anchors. MAY be omitted entirely; an empty array is also valid. See §3.5.

reference.description: The anchor reference (PDA address, tx hash, timestamp blob, ...). Opaque to the protocol.

chain.description: Network identifier when the anchor type is chain-based.

authority.description: Issuing authority when the anchor type is authority-based (e.g. RFC 3161 TSA URL).
